title: rubus flagellaris var. inermis
intro: Rubus flagellaris var. inermis, also known as the thimbleberry or thimble bush, is a variety of the black raspberry plant native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus flagellaris var. inermis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are also used in jams and preserves for its flavor and nutritional value.
